author: Dr. Lizard
title: Why do males and females have equal PNH incidence despite X-linked PIGA mutations?
answer: PIGA mutations are somatic, not germline, and X-inactivation in females allows single-copy mutations to cause disease.
detail: PIGA gene is located on the X chromosome at position Xp22.2 in hematopoietic stem cells.
Males need only one X chromosome mutation since they have a single X chromosome per cell.
Females undergo random X-inactivation early in development, leaving only one active X chromosome per cell.
If the mutated X is active in a stem cell, that cell becomes PNH-positive regardless of sex.
The mutation occurs somatically in blood stem cells, not in germline, so it is not inherited.
releaseNote: PIGA mutations in PNH are somatic, not germline, and X-inactivation in females allows single-copy mutations to cause disease, resulting in equal PNH incidence in males and females.
